deviations.what: colleague is dropped from both the implementer and the reviewer role in this fan-out; Claude subagents implement, and independent review comes from SonarCloud plus Qodo on each PR
deviations.reason: The approved gate-2 split plan assigned t73/t75/t81-t83 to 'ask-colleague write --apply' and put 'ask-colleague review' on every task diff. Two consecutive colleague drives on well-scoped READ-ONLY tasks (explore 923ceb0bd061, review b507f26c5c80) both ended status=incomplete with steps=0, never calling finish. In each the local vllm-openai Qwen3.6-27B emitted a tool call as literal assistant text ('<read_file path=...>' and '<tool_call> run_command:') instead of a structured call. Notably 'colleague doctor --probe' reports tool_calling OK, so the probe is not representative of the drive path. A backend that completes zero steps on a read-only audit cannot be trusted to implement a CI gate or to review a security-relevant diff.

deviations.what: t72 and t73 start from partially-complete work rather than from scratch: CLAUDE.md was already rewritten and scripts/colleague_inventory.py already authored during the spec phase, so each task is reduced to its remaining delta
deviations.reason: The plan was written after the /scope->/think->/challenge->/spec-to-plan arc had already produced the CLAUDE.md operations-plane rewrite and the pinned AST inventory scanner as spec evidence. Treating t72/t73 as greenfield would mean deleting and re-authoring committed-quality work to satisfy the plan's narrative. t72's remaining delta is the issue #1 six-part plan post plus AGENTS.colleague.md/README.md alignment and the artifact-trail commit; t73's remaining delta is wiring scripts/colleague_inventory.py --check into tests.yml as a known-debt gate publishing debt_remaining.

deviations.what: PR ordering is forced to strict sequence rather than the plan's parallel wave 1: t71 alone, then t72, then t73
deviations.reason: publish.yml triggers on the pyproject.toml path and every PR must bump the version, so EVERY merge publishes to PyPI. The plan's wave 1 would have run t72 and t73 in parallel with the artifact-trail commit, but any merge landing before t71 would publish an unhardened release to an append-only index. t71 therefore merges strictly first and alone. This narrows realised parallelism at the start of the run but is required by t71's own acceptance criterion that it merge first.

deviations.what: colleague's zero-step failure is INTERMITTENT, not deterministic: a third read-only drive succeeded and produced usable review findings, so d1's conclusion is corrected from unusable to unreliable-but-worth-asking when read-only
deviations.reason: d1 recorded colleague as unusable on the evidence of two consecutive drives completing zero steps (923ceb0bd061, b507f26c5c80). A third drive (8c6f080d4d56) on a comparable read-only review task completed normally, answered four verification questions with accurate file:line references, and raised three observations — two independently corroborating an adversarial pass, one a fair critique of a test written during this run (fragile row filter, since rewritten to select structurally). Roughly one in three drives returned usable work. The corrected conclusion: not reliable enough to place in a critical path, but worth asking when the request is read-only and a failure costs only the wait. Graded 4/5.

deviations.what: t69/t70 filed as PRIVATE GitHub security advisories (GHSA-6pfg-9vcp-hqw4, GHSA-2m42-prxw-43w6), not public issues
deviations.reason: colleague is a PUBLIC repo released on PyPI at 1.51.0. A public issue describing a policy-guard bypass with no fix available is a zero-day disclosure. t69's own acceptance criterion requires private-fix-then-disclose. The communicate skill's default channel (post-issue.sh) is wrong for this content.

deviations.what: t74 split: t74a (six schemas + behavioural fixtures) proceeds NOW at SHA 28fee29; t74b (policy-composition fixtures) holds for the fixed colleague release
deviations.reason: VERIFIED: colleague/tools.py contains zero references to policy (grep finds no match). t69 owns policy.py/loop.py/worktrees.py/subagents.py/work.py; t70 owns policy.py/subagents.py. Neither touches tools.py, so the security lane provably cannot change SCHEMAS[:6] or the six handlers. The 'never encode pre-hardening composition as parity' criterion is scoped to composition fixtures, which are held.

deviations.what: t76 foundation runs PARALLEL to t74a rather than sequentially behind it
deviations.reason: t76's dependency on t74 is methodological (capture the parity target before building, so parity is not shaped around the implementation), not a content dependency: the Operation/OperationResult/Environment types are provider-neutral by mandate and consume no fixture. Running in separate worktrees preserves that intent - the t74a agent reads only colleague and never sees t76's work.

deviations.what: Version bump + CHANGELOG entry are performed by the MERGING agent at local-merge time, not by each task agent
deviations.reason: The per-task INVARIANT says every PR bumps the version. pyproject.toml and CHANGELOG.md are shared by every task in the plan, so N parallel worktree agents each bumping them guarantees a three-way conflict on every merge. The invariant is still satisfied per merge - ownership moves, not the requirement.

deviations.what: Fan-out slices land as LOCAL merges to main, not GitHub pull requests
deviations.reason: Operator directive: subagent takes a task, works in a worktree, the merging agent merges it locally to main, fan-out continues. GitHub PR overhead is not wanted for intra-fanout slices.

deviations.what: t78 (policy evaluator port) proceeds WITHOUT waiting for colleague's security fixes, excluding parent/child composition semantics
deviations.reason: SETTLED FROM THE SPEC, not assumed. c26 states policy.py:79-351 is ~270 lines of dependency-free stdlib 'requiring zero changes', and only load_policy:354-400 needs a seam. c11 states the evaluator and data structures move while location resolution does NOT. GHSA-6pfg-9vcp-hqw4 is a RESOLUTION fix (where approvals.json is read from); the evaluator being ported does not resolve. c12's source-root snapshot is shell-cli's own Environment design. EXCLUDED: parent/child policy composition, which GHSA-2m42-prxw-43w6 changes.
